notes: Gold DQ rules for consumable.regional_price_parities. Mirrors the Silver 39-rule structure scaled to the 15-column Gold schema with 4 new derivations (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) and 1 carry-forward (verification_status per Bronze Condition 7). Every rule populates the top-level `category` field consumed by brightsmith.infra.dq_runner (closes MEDIUM-3 from Bronze audit). `dimension` is retained as a synonym for continuity. Thresholds are sourced from the analytical dry-run in governance/eda/gold-regional-price-parities-eda.md (51 rows derived, 0 anomalies, all 8 spot checks pass with ZERO delta, inverse invariant max deviation 1.42e-14, adjusted_Nk derivation purity = 0.0 delta on all 51 rows, cost_tier boundary witness TN at rpp=91.0 exactly). The adjusted_Nk tolerances are held at the spec's 0.01 despite observed 0.0 delta (same robustness-margin philosophy as Silver's inverse invariant). The 8 BEA-verified spot checks are encoded as individual P0 rules pinning (state_abbr, cost_tier, adjusted_50k, verification_status) against the exact EDA values. The cost_tier classification correctness rule re-runs the canonical CASE expression in SQL and asserts zero mismatches. The TN boundary witness rule is a dedicated P0 rule hardening the left-closed semantics. The passthrough integrity rule joins Gold to Silver on state_fips and flags any difference in the 8 carry-forward columns; it is marked evaluation_mode=production_only and chaos_exclude=true mirroring the Silver SIL-BEA-018 cross-zone shadow limitation (the DQ runner's shadow rewrite stages only a shadow_consumable copy during Gold chaos, not shadow_base). Current verification state is 8 bea_official / 43 estimate mirroring Silver; the COUNT=8 rule flips to COUNT=51 when the live BEA API refresh lands, in lockstep with SIL-BEA-023.

rules.name: Row count exactly 51 (50 states + DC)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: consumable.regional_price_parities must contain exactly 51 rows — the closed set of 50 U.S. states plus the District of Columbia. The Gold transformer is a row-for-row derivation from the 51-row Silver table; any deviation indicates a dedup bug, a dropped row, or a double-load.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Record Count: 51 (dry-run derived in-memory from 51 Silver rows)'. Physical model declares 'Expected row count: Exactly 51 rows (50 states + DC). Closed set.' Zero violations observed. Threshold = 51 exactly.

rules.name: state_fips non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_fips IS NULL
rules.description: state_fips is the natural key of consumable.regional_price_parities and drives every lookup and join into this table.
rules.rationale: EDA state_fips field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'state_fips VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_fips uniqueness (natural key)
rules.sql: SELECT state_fips, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.regional_price_parities GROUP BY state_fips HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: state_fips is the declared dedup grain of consumable.regional_price_parities. Any duplicate indicates a promote-pattern dedup failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: '51 distinct (100% unique)'. Physical model: 'Natural key: state_fips (UNIQUE, NOT NULL)' and 'Dedup grain: [state_fips]'. Threshold: 0 duplicate state_fips values.

rules.name: state_fips format: 2-digit zero-padded numeric string
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE NOT regexp_matches(state_fips, '^[0-9]{2}$')
rules.description: state_fips must be a 2-character zero-padded numeric string (e.g., '06' California, '11' DC). Catches float coercion regressions where '06' decays to '6' or '6.0'.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'All 51 values match ^\d{2}$. Passes.' Physical model: 'CHECK (state_fips ~ \'^\\d{2}$\')'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_fips in canonical 51-member FIPS set
rules.description: Every row's state_fips must be in the canonical 51-member set of FIPS codes assigned to the 50 U.S. states plus DC (FIPS 11). The set intentionally contains gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, and 52 — those codes are unassigned in the federal FIPS standard. A format-only regex is insufficient because it would admit bogus codes like '99', '03', or '43'. Mirrors SIL-BEA-039 which closed Silver chaos Gap 2.
rules.rationale: Silver closed this gap via SIL-BEA-039 after chaos probe E6 (Alaska state_fips='99' went undetected by regex alone). Gold must assert the same canonical closed set. EDA: state_fips distinct count = 51, all values present in the canonical NIST FIPS 5-2 state+DC allocation. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_name non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_name IS NULL
rules.description: state_name is the human-readable display name required by the frontend and the data contract.
rules.rationale: EDA state_name field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). 51 distinct (100% unique).' Physical model: 'state_name VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_fips ↔ state_name bijection (equal distinct counts)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_name) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Every state_fips must map to exactly one state_name and vice versa. Bijection failure means either a duplicate state_name or a drift in the 51-row expected set.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Bijection checks': 'state_fips ↔ state_name: 51 distinct fips, 51 distinct names, 1:1.' Mirrors SIL-BEA-006. Threshold: both counts = 51.

rules.name: state_abbr non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_abbr IS NULL
rules.description: state_abbr is the USPS 2-letter code the frontend and MCP tools use as the primary state key.
rules.rationale: EDA state_abbr field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'state_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_abbr format: exactly 2 uppercase letters
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE NOT regexp_matches(state_abbr, '^[A-Z]{2}$')
rules.description: USPS abbreviations are always 2 uppercase ASCII letters.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'All 51 values match ^[A-Z]{2}$.' Physical model: 'CHECK (state_abbr ~ \'^[A-Z]{2}$\')'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_abbr value set equals canonical USPS-51
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_abbr NOT IN ('AK','AL','AR','AZ','CA','CO','CT','DC','DE','FL','GA','HI','IA','ID','IL','IN','KS','KY','LA','MA','MD','ME','MI','MN','MO','MS','MT','NC','ND','NE','NH','NJ','NM','NV','NY','OH','OK','OR','PA','RI','SC','SD','TN','TX','UT','VA','VT','WA','WI','WV','WY')
rules.description: state_abbr must come from the canonical 51-member USPS set (50 states + DC). Rejects unexpected territory codes (PR, GU, VI, AS, MP).
rules.rationale: Mirrors SIL-BEA-009. EDA: state_abbr values match the canonical 51-member set exactly. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_abbr uniqueness (51 distinct)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_abbr) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: 51 rows must yield 51 distinct USPS abbreviations.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'state_abbr ... 51 distinct (100% unique)'. Physical model: 'UNIQUE (state_abbr)'. Threshold: 51 distinct.

rules.name: state_fips ↔ state_abbr bijection (equal distinct counts)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_abbr) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Every state_fips must map to exactly one state_abbr and vice versa.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Bijection checks': 'state_fips ↔ state_abbr: 51 distinct fips, 51 distinct abbrs, 1:1.' Mirrors SIL-BEA-011. Threshold: both counts = 51.

rules.name: census_region non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE census_region IS NULL
rules.description: census_region is a required carry-forward from Silver.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'census_region VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: census_region values IN enum {Northeast, Midwest, South, West}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE census_region NOT IN ('Northeast','Midwest','South','West')
rules.description: census_region is a closed 4-value enum per the U.S. Census Bureau convention. DC is intentionally placed in 'South' per Census quirk.
rules.rationale: EDA: '4 distinct (Northeast, Midwest, South, West)'. Physical model: 'CHECK (census_region IN (...))'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: All 4 census regions represented
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT census_region) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 4 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: All four U.S. Census regions must appear in the output. Catches a regression where an entire region's states drop.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'South=17, West=13, Midwest=12, Northeast=9. Total=51.' Threshold: 4 distinct regions.

rules.name: rpp_all_items non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE rpp_all_items IS NULL
rules.description: rpp_all_items is the core measure driving every Gold derivation (cost_tier, adjusted_Nk). Null breaks all downstream calculations.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'rpp_all_items DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: rpp_all_items in range [70.0, 130.0]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE rpp_all_items < 70.0 OR rpp_all_items > 130.0
rules.description: Gold sanity bound for the RPP index on the national=100 scale, matching the Silver bound.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'rpp_all_items ranges [86.9, 110.7], well inside [70.0, 130.0].' Physical model: 'CHECK (rpp_all_items BETWEEN 70.0 AND 130.0)'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: purchasing_power_multiplier non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE purchasing_power_multiplier IS NULL
rules.description: purchasing_power_multiplier is the pre-computed salary scaling factor used by every adjusted_Nk derivation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). Cardinality: 51 distinct.' Physical model: 'purchasing_power_multiplier DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: purchasing_power_multiplier in range [0.7, 1.3]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE purchasing_power_multiplier < 0.7 OR purchasing_power_multiplier > 1.3
rules.description: Gold sanity bound on the pre-computed salary multiplier. The bound [0.7, 1.3] is the inverse of the rpp_all_items bound [70, 130] / 100.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'purchasing_power_multiplier ranges [0.9033, 1.1507].' Physical model: 'CHECK (purchasing_power_multiplier BETWEEN 0.7 AND 1.3)'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Inverse invariant: multiplier × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0 (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE abs(purchasing_power_multiplier * rpp_all_items - 100.0) > 0.01
rules.description: Mathematical derivation invariant: the multiplier is defined as 100.0 / rpp_all_items, so multiplier × rpp_all_items must equal 100.0 for every row. Tolerance of 0.01 is a deliberate robustness margin — the observed Gold deviation is 1.42e-14.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Max absolute deviation of ppm × rpp from 100.0: 1.42e-14. Rows violating 0.01 tolerance: 0.' Twelve orders of magnitude below threshold. Any real derivation bug would shift results by far more than 0.01. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: cost_tier non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier IS NULL
rules.description: cost_tier is derived at Gold via a CASE expression with a final ELSE branch, so it cannot legitimately be null. Null means the transformer wrote a null before the derivation was applied.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'cost_tier Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). 5 distinct values.' Physical model: 'cost_tier VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: cost_tier values IN enum {very_high, high, average, low, very_low}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier NOT IN ('very_high','high','average','low','very_low')
rules.description: cost_tier is a closed 5-value enum per the frozen BT-106 definition. The CASE expression in the transformer can only emit these 5 values; any other value means either the transformer was hand-edited or the column was corrupted post-write.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'cost_tier distribution: very_high=4, high=8, average=13, low=11, very_low=15. All 5 distinct values materialize.' Physical model: 'CHECK (cost_tier IN (\'very_high\',\'high\',\'average\',\'low\',\'very_low\'))'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: cost_tier classification correctness (re-run CASE expression)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier <> CASE WHEN rpp_all_items >= 108.0 THEN 'very_high' WHEN rpp_all_items >= 103.0 THEN 'high' WHEN rpp_all_items >= 97.0 THEN 'average' WHEN rpp_all_items >= 91.0 THEN 'low' ELSE 'very_low' END
rules.description: For every row, the persisted cost_tier must equal the result of the canonical CASE expression applied to the row's rpp_all_items. The SQL re-runs the frozen classifier (breakpoints 108, 103, 97, 91, left-closed) and counts mismatches. This is the single most important derivation-correctness rule at Gold — it catches any breakpoint drift, any tier-label typo, any off-by-one in the left-closed semantics, and any boundary-flip regression on the 17 states that sit within 1.0 RPP of a breakpoint.
rules.rationale: Physical model 'Cost Tier Derivation' section freezes this exact CASE expression (breakpoints 108.0, 103.0, 97.0, 91.0, left-closed). EDA 'cost_tier ↔ rpp_all_items: Perfect monotonic mapping by construction. Breakpoints observed in effect: the very_low / low boundary hits exactly at TN rpp=91.0 (TN is low; OH at 90.8 is very_low), confirming the left-closed convention.' 17 states are within 1.0 RPP of a breakpoint — the biggest chaos-target surface at Gold. Threshold: 0 mismatches.

rules.name: cost_tier left-closed boundary witness: TN at rpp=91.0 is 'low'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_fips = '47' AND (abs(rpp_all_items - 91.0) > 1e-9 OR cost_tier <> 'low')
rules.description: Tennessee sits exactly on the 91.0 breakpoint between 'very_low' and 'low'. Under the frozen left-closed convention (`rpp >= 91.0` → 'low'), TN must be classified as 'low', not 'very_low'. This is a dedicated witness rule for the left-closed semantics: any accidental swap to right-closed (`rpp > 91.0`) would flip TN from 'low' to 'very_low' and this rule would fire. Hardens GLD-RPP-023 against a specific regression that is otherwise hard to spot.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Boundary Proximity' table: 'TN | 47 | 91.0 | breakpoint 91.0 | +0.00 | low | estimate | Exact boundary hit. Left-closed convention puts TN in low. Single biggest chaos target.' EDA chaos recommendations rank TN as target #1: 'TN is the single most important target — sitting exactly on 91.0, it is the witness row for the left-closed convention. Any accidental swap to right-closed would move TN from low → very_low.' Spec breakpoint semantics: `WHEN rpp_all_items >= 91.0 THEN 'low'`. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: adjusted_30k non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE adjusted_30k IS NULL
rules.description: adjusted_30k is derived at Gold from a non-null purchasing_power_multiplier and cannot legitimately be null.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'adjusted_30k Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'adjusted_30k DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: adjusted_30k derivation purity (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE abs(adjusted_30k - round(30000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)) > 0.01
rules.description: Every adjusted_30k must equal `round(30000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)` within 0.01. Robustness tolerance kept at spec value despite EDA max delta = 0.0.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Derivation is pure and deterministic. adjusted_30k = round(30000.0 × ppm, 2) for all 51 rows with delta 0.0.' Physical model: 'CHECK (abs(adjusted_30k - round(30000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)) <= 0.01)'. Threshold: 0 violations at tol 0.01.

rules.name: adjusted_50k non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE adjusted_50k IS NULL
rules.description: adjusted_50k is the canonical salary benchmark used by the frontend salary-adjustment UI.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'adjusted_50k Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'adjusted_50k DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: adjusted_50k derivation purity (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE abs(adjusted_50k - round(50000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)) > 0.01
rules.description: Every adjusted_50k must equal `round(50000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)` within 0.01. Robustness tolerance kept at spec value despite EDA max delta = 0.0.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'adjusted_50k == round(50000.0 × ppm, 2) for all 51 rows with delta 0.0. Distribution: min=45167.12 (CA), max=57537.40 (AR), mean=51820.84, median=51599.59 (TX).' Physical model CHECK constraint. Threshold: 0 violations at tol 0.01.

rules.name: adjusted_75k non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE adjusted_75k IS NULL
rules.description: adjusted_75k is a non-null Gold derivation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'adjusted_75k Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'adjusted_75k DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: adjusted_75k derivation purity (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE abs(adjusted_75k - round(75000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)) > 0.01
rules.description: Every adjusted_75k must equal `round(75000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)` within 0.01.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Same derivation purity holds for adjusted_75k (delta = 0.0 for all 51 rows).' Physical model CHECK constraint. Threshold: 0 violations at tol 0.01.

rules.name: adjusted_100k non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE adjusted_100k IS NULL
rules.description: adjusted_100k is a non-null Gold derivation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'adjusted_100k Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'adjusted_100k DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: adjusted_100k derivation purity (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE abs(adjusted_100k - round(100000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)) > 0.01
rules.description: Every adjusted_100k must equal `round(100000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)` within 0.01.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Same derivation purity holds for adjusted_100k (delta = 0.0 for all 51 rows).' Physical model CHECK constraint. Threshold: 0 violations at tol 0.01.

rules.name: CA high-cost sanity: adjusted_50k < 50000
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_fips = '06' AND adjusted_50k >= 50000.0
rules.description: California is a high-cost state (rpp_all_items=110.7, ppm<1.0), so adjusted_50k must be strictly less than $50,000. Catches any sign-flip or inversion in the multiplier.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'CA sanity holds: adjusted_50k = 45167.12 < 50000.0.' Spec: 'CA adjusted_50k < 50000 (high-cost sanity).' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: IA low-cost sanity: adjusted_50k > 50000
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_fips = '19' AND adjusted_50k <= 50000.0
rules.description: Iowa is a low-cost state (rpp_all_items=87.8, ppm>1.0), so adjusted_50k must be strictly greater than $50,000. Paired with GLD-RPP-033 this proves the multiplier is oriented correctly.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'IA sanity holds: adjusted_50k = 56947.61 > 50000.0.' Spec: 'IA adjusted_50k > 50000 (low-cost sanity).' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: verification_status values IN enum {bea_official, estimate}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE verification_status NOT IN ('bea_official','estimate')
rules.description: verification_status is a closed 2-value enum carried forward from Silver per Bronze staff-review Condition 7. Surfaces per-row provenance on every Gold row.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'verification_status distribution: bea_official=8, estimate=43. 2 distinct values.' Physical model: 'CHECK (verification_status IN (\'bea_official\',\'estimate\'))'. Condition 7 carry-forward. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Exactly 8 rows are verification_status = 'bea_official'
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE verification_status = 'bea_official') = 8 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The current Silver verification state has exactly 8 BEA-verified rows (CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK). Gold must honor that contract and not claim more or less verification than Silver. When the live BEA API refresh lands post-hackathon, this rule flips to '= 51' in lockstep with SIL-BEA-023.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'verification_status: bea_official=8 (15.69%), estimate=43 (84.31%).' Condition 7 carry-forward: Gold must mirror the Silver count exactly. Mirrors SIL-BEA-023. Threshold: count = 8.

rules.name: Every bea_official row's state_fips is in the 8-state allow-list
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE verification_status = 'bea_official' AND state_fips NOT IN ('05','06','11','15','19','28','34','40')
rules.description: The 8 BEA-verified state_fips values are a fixed allow-list: {'05','06','11','15','19','28','34','40'} (AR, CA, DC, HI, IA, MS, NJ, OK). Any other FIPS with verification_status='bea_official' at Gold means the Condition 7 carry-forward has drifted from Silver.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'bea_official FIPS set at Gold: [05,06,11,15,19,28,34,40]. Match with Silver allow-list: exact.' Mirrors SIL-BEA-024. Physical model re-asserts this as a P0 DQ rule at Gold. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: record_id non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE record_id IS NULL
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic surrogate key produced by compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). Null means the grain ID helper failed.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'record_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY'. EDA: 'record_id Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Format: 'rpc-<16 hex chars>'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: record_id uniqueness (surrogate key)
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.regional_price_parities GROUP BY record_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: record_id is the primary key of consumable.regional_price_parities. Duplicate means either a state_fips collision or a hash collision (extraordinarily unlikely with SHA-256).
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'PRIMARY KEY (record_id)' and 'Deterministic surrogate key: compute_grain_id(row, [state_fips], prefix=rpc)'. EDA: 'record_id 51 distinct (100% unique).' Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: record_id format: rpc-<16 hex chars>
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE NOT regexp_matches(record_id, '^rpc-[0-9a-f]{16}$')
rules.description: record_id must follow the canonical `rpc-` prefix format (16 lowercase hex chars). The `rpc` prefix distinguishes Gold consumable record_ids from Silver's `rpp-` prefix — any row with a mismatched prefix means the compute_grain_id call was passed the wrong prefix argument.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'Format: rpc-<16 hex chars>. Prefix rpc distinguishes Gold consumable record_ids from Silver rpp- prefix.' EDA confirms all 51 values match. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: data_year equals 2024
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE data_year <> 2024
rules.description: This Gold table mirrors the Silver single-vintage contract. Every row must carry data_year=2024. When the BEA releases a new vintage, Bronze/Silver/Gold rules bump to the new target year in lockstep.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'data_year distinct values: 1 (2024)'. Physical model: 'CHECK (data_year = 2024)'. Mirror of SIL-BEA-027. Threshold: 0 rows with data_year != 2024.

rules.name: COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1 (supersession contract)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: consumable.regional_price_parities uses full-table-replacement supersession, not SCD2. At any point in time the table must contain exactly one data_year. Multiple years means either the replacement strategy broke or rows from a stale load leaked through.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'Supersession: Full-table replacement on refresh. Not SCD2. Matches Silver.' EDA: 'Single data year: all 51 rows have data_year = 2024.' Mirrors SIL-BEA-028. Threshold: exactly 1 distinct year.

rules.name: Passthrough integrity: Gold carry-forward columns equal Silver
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities g LEFT JOIN base.bea_rpp s ON g.state_fips = s.state_fips WHERE s.state_fips IS NULL OR g.state_name <> s.state_name OR g.state_abbr <> s.state_abbr OR g.census_region <> s.census_region OR ABS(g.rpp_all_items - s.rpp_all_items) > 1e-9 OR ABS(g.purchasing_power_multiplier - s.purchasing_power_multiplier) > 1e-9 OR g.verification_status <> s.verification_status OR g.data_year <> s.data_year
rules.description: Each of {state_name, state_abbr, census_region, rpp_all_items, purchasing_power_multiplier, verification_status, data_year} in Gold must equal the Silver row's value for the same state_fips (joined to the Gold-native state_fips itself, which is implicitly covered by the INNER resolution). Gold is a pure passthrough for these 8 columns (state_fips included as the join key); the 4 added columns (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) are derivations and are validated separately by GLD-RPP-023, GLD-RPP-026, GLD-RPP-028, GLD-RPP-030, GLD-RPP-032. The join returns all Gold rows LEFT-joined to Silver and counts any row where (a) the Silver match is missing or (b) any carried-forward column differs. Floating-point columns use a 1e-9 tolerance to avoid spurious diffs from DOUBLE round-trips (same tolerance as SIL-BEA-018).
rules.rationale: Physical model source-to-target mapping: rows 2-7, 13, 14 all marked 'Direct passthrough' from base.bea_rpp. Any divergence would indicate the Gold transformer is silently rewriting columns it should not touch. Count-form SQL so the runner returns a single integer that must equal 0. Mirrors the remediated SIL-BEA-018 pattern with the production_only/chaos_exclude carve-out for the same cross-zone shadow limitation. Threshold: 0 mismatched or missing rows.
rules.chaos_exclude_reason: Cross-zone rule: joins consumable.regional_price_parities (Gold) against base.bea_rpp (Silver). The brightsmith DQ runner's shadow-mode rewrite (brightsmith.infra.dq_runner._register_iceberg_views, shadow=True) rewrites ALL known-namespace refs to shadow_<ns>, but the Gold chaos harness only stages a shadow_consumable copy and never stages a shadow_base copy. Consequently the shadow_base.bea_rpp view registration fails and this rule ERRORs on every shadow invocation including the noop negative control (same root cause as Silver chaos Gap 3 for SIL-BEA-018). The rule is correct and passes in production; chaos runners must filter it out post-hoc via rule_id or by honoring this evaluation_mode marker. A future cross-zone shadow mode in the runner would let us drop this carve-out.

rules.name: Spot check: CA (FIPS 06) — abbr=CA, tier=very_high, adjusted_50k=45167.12, bea_official
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE state_fips = '06' AND (state_abbr <> 'CA' OR cost_tier <> 'very_high' OR abs(adjusted_50k - 45167.12) > 0.01 OR verification_status <> 'bea_official')
rules.description: California spot check against the spec's BEA-verified value table. rpp_all_items=110.7 → cost_tier='very_high', adjusted_50k=$45,167.12.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'CA | 06 | very_high | very_high | 45167.12 | 45167.12 | 0.0000 | bea_official | PASS'. All 4 conjuncts verified with ZERO delta. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: All 5 cost_tier values materialize
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT cost_tier) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities) = 5 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: All 5 cost_tier values ('very_high','high','average','low','very_low') must be present in the output. Soft coverage guarantee: the current data has 51 states spread across all 5 tiers and the distribution is stable enough that any refresh that collapses to 4 tiers is worth investigating. Not pinned to exact per-tier counts because estimates may shift on refresh.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Distinct cost_tier values materialized: 5 (100% of spec-defined). Tighten P1 from at least 3 to = 5.' EDA distribution: very_high=4, high=8, average=13, low=11, very_low=15. The full 5-tier coverage is evidence-backed across all 51 states. Threshold: 5 distinct cost_tiers.

rules.name: Each cost_tier has at least 1 row
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier = 'very_high') >= 1 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier = 'high') >= 1 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier = 'average') >= 1 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier = 'low') >= 1 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE cost_tier = 'very_low') >= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Explicit per-tier coverage check: each of the 5 cost_tier values must have at least 1 row. Complementary to GLD-RPP-052's distinct-count rule — this one surfaces which specific tier is empty, rather than just that total distinct < 5.
rules.rationale: EDA distribution: very_high=4, high=8, average=13, low=11, very_low=15. Every tier has at least 4 rows today; the >=1 floor is deliberately loose to tolerate refresh drift at the breakpoints without flagging every move. Per task input: 'do not pin cost_tier distribution to exact counts; use each-tier-has->=1-row P1.' Threshold: all 5 tiers have >= 1 row.

rules.name: promoted_at non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.regional_price_parities WHERE promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: promoted_at is the Gold promotion timestamp. Non-null only; never pinned to a specific value because it changes every run. Replaces Silver's ingested_at/source_load_date metadata pair.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'promoted_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL. Generated at transformation time via datetime.now().' Mirrors SIL-BEA-030 on the Gold-native metadata column. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: Source Silver freshness: base.bea_rpp loaded within 400 days
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT MAX(source_load_date) FROM base.bea_rpp) >= (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 400 DAY) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The upstream Silver table base.bea_rpp must have been loaded within the last 400 days. This enforces a soft vintage staleness bound: BEA publishes RPPs on an annual cadence (roughly December of year N+1 for vintage N), so 400 days covers one full vintage cycle plus a 35-day grace period. Any value > 400 days stale means a vintage refresh has been missed.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'Upstream: base.bea_rpp (Silver).' EDA confirms Silver source_load_date = 2026-04-10 (current). BEA RPP release cadence is annual. 400 days = 365 + 35-day grace. Marked P1 because a single missed refresh is operationally important but not catastrophic (data remains correct for the prior vintage). Threshold: MAX(source_load_date) within 400 days of CURRENT_DATE.
rules.chaos_exclude_reason: Cross-zone rule: reads base.bea_rpp (Silver) from the Gold DQ suite. Same shadow-mode limitation as GLD-RPP-043 and SIL-BEA-018: the Gold chaos harness stages only shadow_consumable, not shadow_base, so this rule ERRORs under shadow invocation. Correct and passes in production.
